title: Gravity
definition: The fundamental force of attraction between all masses in the universe, described mathematically by Newton and later as spacetime curvature by Einstein.

difficulty_levels.beginner: Gravity is the invisible pull that keeps us on the ground and makes planets orbit around stars.
difficulty_levels.intermediate: Gravity is a fundamental force responsible for large-scale structure in the universe, from planetary motion to galaxy formation.
difficulty_levels.expert: Gravity is a curvature of spacetime caused by mass and energy, according to Einstein's general relativity. It governs cosmic structures but is the weakest of the four fundamental forces.

extra_fields.insight: Gravity is not just a force, but a curve in the fabric of space and time.
